Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The advantage of slotted antennas in modern radar technology is to…
produce a wide beam for better target detection.
greatly reduce lateral lobes, consequently concentrating more energy into the main beam.
eliminate the need for azimuth slaving.
dogru: greatly reduce lateral lobes, consequently concentrating more energy into the main beam.

soru:
simultaneously transmit weather and mapping beams.
Considering a primary radar system, what kind of aerials are used?
secenekler: An omnidirectional antenna for transmitting, and a directional antenna for receiving.
A directional antenna for transmitting, and an omnidirectional antenna for receiving.
One directional antenna both for transmitting and for receiving.
One directional antenna for transmitting and one for receiving.
dogru: One directional antenna both for transmitting and for receiving.

soru: Comparing a parabolic reflector with a flat plate antenna of the same size
secenekler: the parabolic reflector has a considerably smaller beam width.
the parabolic reflector generates less side lobes than the flat plate antenna.
the flat plate antenna has a considerably smaller beam width.
the flat plate antenna generates less side lobes than the parabolic reflector.
dogru: the flat plate antenna generates less side lobes than the parabolic reflector.

soru: Which aeronautical radio frequency band uses refraction within ionospheric layers?
Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: An apparent increase in the transmitted frequency, which is proportional to the transmitter velocity, will occur when the…
receiver moves away from the transmitter.
transmitter moves away from the receiver.

soru:
transmitter goes into standby.
Due to ‘Doppler’ effect an apparent decrease in the transmitted frequency, which is proportional to the transmitter’s velocity, will occur when:
secenekler: the transmitter and receiver move towards each other.
the transmitter moves away from the receiver.
there is no relative movement between the transmitter and the receiver.
the transmitter moves toward the receiver.
dogru: the transmitter moves away from the receiver.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: wavelength distance of a certain frequency.
range from the transmitter to the first sky wave return.
dogru: range from the transmitter to the first sky wave return.

soru:
highest critical frequency distance.
In the propagation of MF waves, the phenomenon of FADING is particularly found:
secenekler: by day, due to the combination of sky and ground waves.
at night, due to the combination of the sky and ground waves.
dogru: at night, due to the combination of the sky and ground waves.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: The skip distance of HF-transmission will increase with…
higher frequency and lower level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
higher frequency and higher level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
lower frequency and higher level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
dogru: higher frequency and higher level of the refracting ionospheric layer.

soru:
lower frequency and lower level of the refracting ionospheric layer.
The wavelength of a non-directional beacon (NDB) with a frequency of 300 kHz is:
With regard to types of transmission, which of the following statements is true:
secenekler: Pulse transmission is suitable only for NDB's
Continuous Wave transmission varies only in amplitude
In pulse transmission the frequency is modulated to facilitate range measurement
Continuous Wave transmission can be either frequency or amplitude modulated
dogru: Continuous Wave transmission can be either frequency or amplitude modulated

soru: Attenuation of radio waves means:
secenekler: Only the absorbtion of radio energy by the sea
The atmospheric bending of the waves
The weakening of the radiated waves
Only the scattering of the waves by the Tropopause
dogru: The weakening of the radiated waves

soru: The VHF frequency band has a wavelength limit of:

Refer to image In the diagram, which refers to the propagation of HF radio waves, the letters A, B, C, D and E correctly represent:
View Annex 062-005
secenekler: A Dead space     B Skip dist          C Sky wave          D Ground wave       E Critical angle
A Skip dist           B Dead space    C Ground wave    D Sky wave     E Critical angle
A Sky wave        B Dead space    C Ground wave    D Skip dist    E Critical angle
A Dead space     B Skip dist          C Ground wave    D Critical angle      E Sky wave
dogru: A Sky wave        B Dead space    C Ground wave    D Skip dist    E Critical angle

soru: Modulation is:
secenekler: The addition of a low frequency signal (tone, voice) to a high frequency carrier wave
The addition of a high frequency signal (tone, voice) to a low frequency carrier wave
Tuning receiver into the frequency of the transmitter
Elimination of disturbances for improving reception
dogru: The addition of a low frequency signal (tone, voice) to a high frequency carrier wave

soru: The phenomenon of a change in direction of an EM-wave occurring due to a change in its speed is called:
Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: In aviation electronic systems the so-called Doppler principle may be used in
VOR, MTI and the mapping mode of AWR.
VOR, GPS and MTI and the turbulence mode of AWR.
dogru: VOR, GPS and MTI and the turbulence mode of AWR.

soru:
VOR , DME and GPS.
What is the Doppler effect with reference to radio signals?
secenekler: It is the shift in frequency of the radio wave due to the relative movement between transmitter and receiver
It is interference between the direct and the earth reflected wave.
It is fluctuations in their propagation speed.
It is the increases or decreases in the ground speed of the transmitting aircraft.
dogru: It is the shift in frequency of the radio wave due to the relative movement between transmitter and receiver

soru: Which of the following summaries lists only directional antennae?
secenekler: Dipole antenna, sense antenna, parabolic antenna, helical antenna.
Dipole antenna, loop antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna.
Sense antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna, helical antenna.
Loop antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna, helical antenna.
dogru: Loop antenna, parabolic antenna, slotted planar array antenna, helical antenna.

soru: Given a wire fed with alternating current and a second wire parallel to, but remote from it. The result will be:
secenekler: the first wire will radiate electro-magnetic waves into space and direct current will be induced in the second wire.
the first wire will radiate electro-magnetic waves into space but the transmitted energy will be completely absorbed by the second wire.
the first wire will radiate electro-magnetic waves into space and the second wire will double the transmission power of the first one.
The first wire will radiate electromagnetic waves into space and alternating current will be induced in the second wire
dogru: The first wire will radiate electromagnetic waves into space and alternating current will be induced in the second wire

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: results in a change of amplitude of the carrier wave.
is a modulation form used in GPS where the phase of the carrier wave is reversed.
is a modulation form used in ILS where the phase of the navigation signals is changed with 90° or 150°.
dogru: is a modulation form used in GPS where the phase of the carrier wave is reversed.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: results in a change of polarisation of the modulated signal.
What is meant by keying in A1A modulation
interrupting the carrier wave to break it into dots and dashes.
changing the amplitude of the carrier wave to add information.
dogru: interrupting the carrier wave to break it into dots and dashes.

soru:
adding information on the carrier wave by use of a modulating signal.
interrupting the modulating signal to break it into dots and dashes.

Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: A reason that GPS satellites use helical antennae is
to achieve better omni directional receiving qualities.
to improve the signal to noise ratio.
that the signal has a circular polarization.
dogru: that the signal has a circular polarization.

soru:
to neutralize the ionospheric delay.
What describes polarization?

Orientation of the antenna to the North Pole
Orientation of the plane of oscillation of the magnetic component of the ectromagnetic wave
Orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component
dogru: Orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component

soru: What is the reason that DME utilises pulse pairs instead of single pulses?
secenekler: To be able to use a separate interrogation and reply frequency, and thus to increase the number of DME stations that can transmit in the same part of the DME frequency band.
To increase the accuracy and reliability of the range measurement.
To distinguish DME transmissions from transmissions of other (radar) systems utilising single pulses.
To increase the number of aircraft that can simultaneously use the DME station to determine the DME range.
dogru: To distinguish DME transmissions from transmissions of other (radar) systems utilising single pulses.

soru:
180°

secenekler: A speed just below the speed of sound
A speed depending on the type of electroamgetic waves

soru: Which of the following is true with reference to frequency of a radio wave?
secenekler: Frequency is the length of a radio wave expressed as an angle
Frequency is the number of cycles in one second in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)
Frequency is the minimum number of cycles occurring in one minute in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)
Frequency is the maximum deflection in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)
dogru: Frequency is the number of cycles in one second in a radio wave expressed in Hertz (Hz)

dogru: HF Volmet and HF two-way communication

soru: What describes ‘Polarisation’?
secenekler: The orientation of the plane of oscillation of the magnetic component of the electromagnetic wave.
The rotation of the antenna around a fixed axis.
The orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component of the electromagnetic wave.
The orientation of the antenna to the north pole.
dogru: The orientation of the plane of oscillation of the electrical component of the electromagnetic wave.

soru: The simplest type of antenna construction is a:
secenekler: Dipole antenna which is a wire of length equal to one half of the wavelength
Slotted antenna used in modern weather radars
Parabolic antenna used in weather radars
Loop antenna used in ADF receivers
dogru: Dipole antenna which is a wire of length equal to one half of the wavelength

soru: A Loop Antenna is commonly used in:

secenekler: D, E and F layers and their depth varies with time
A, C and E layers with equal depth
D, E and F layers and their depth does not vary with time
A, B and C layers and their depth varies with time
dogru: D, E and F layers and their depth varies with time

soru: Which statement about VHF/UHF Frequencies is correct?
secenekler: VHF frequencies below 120 MHz may be subjected to sporadic E-reflections and then have sky wave. For both VHF/UHF the sky wave is the main propagation path of practical use
For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practial use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions
Neither VHF/UHF has a sky wave under any conditions. For VHF the surface wave is the main propagation path and for UHF the space wave
VHF has, under normal conditions, a sky wave whereas UHF has not. For both VHF/UHF propagation the space wave is the main propagation path
dogru: For both VHF/UHF propagation, the space wave is the only propagation path of practial use. There is no sky wave under normal conditions

The third symbol indicates the nature of the signal modulating the main carrier
The three symbols together indicate which device is transmitting on the carrier
The first symbol indicates the type of modulation of the main carrier
dogru: The first symbol indicates the type of modulation of the main carrier

soru: The electromagnetic waves refracted from the E and F layers of the ionosphere are called:
An electromagnetic wave consists of an oscillating electric (E) and an oscillating magnetic (H) field. Which of the following answers is true?
secenekler: The E field and the H field are perpendicular to each other.
The H field is parallel to the wire and the E field is perpendicular to it.
When a constant current is sent through the antenna the E and H field are parallel.
A dipole antenna is only capable to transmit the H field.
dogru: The E field and the H field are perpendicular to each other.

soru: Diffraction is the process by which:

secenekler: A wave type transducer for the process of converting a line AC into a free electromagnetic wave.
A transformer for the process of converting AC into a radio wave
A device used for the purpose of receiving a free electromagnetic radio wave and converting it into an audible frequency.
A wave type transducer for the process of converting DC into a free electromagnetic wave.
dogru: A wave type transducer for the process of converting a line AC into a free electromagnetic wave.

soru: With a Doppler VOR system and frequency decreasing:
secenekler: the transmitter and the receiver are moving closer
the transmitter and receiver are stationary

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:
secenekler: A vertically-mounted dipole antenna is used for RF transmissions.The receiving antenna has to be …
aligned horizontally in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the horizontal plane.
in the same vertical alignment in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the vertical plane.
aligned horizontally in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the vertical plane.
dogru: in the same vertical alignment in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the vertical plane.

soru:
in the same vertical alignment in order to receive the electrical field of the RF transmission in the horizontal plane.

secenekler: on both in the upper and lower sidebands
dogru: on both in the upper and lower sidebands

soru: HF "Volmet" broadcasts meteorological information for aircraft in flight by using HF radio transmissions. The characteristics of the HF signal are that:
secenekler: the carrier wave is amplitude modulated and only one of the two sidebands is transmitted (single sideband transmission)
the carrier wave is frequency modulated and only one of the two sidebands is transmitted (single sideband transmission)
the carrier wave is frequency modulated with a modulation index of 16
the carrier wave is amplitude modulated with a fixed modulation depth
dogru: the carrier wave is amplitude modulated and only one of the two sidebands is transmitted (single sideband transmission)

soru: A radio signal is used to establish voice communications between the ground and an aircraft.  This signal is transmitted in A3E. The number "3" means that the:

carrier is modulated by an analogue signal
emitter transmits the complete carrier and the two sidebands
carrier is modulated by a digital information
dogru: carrier is modulated by an analogue signal

soru: A radio signal is transmitted in A2A. These three symbols collectively mean:

an amplitude modulation used for telephony, single sideband, with suppressed carrier
an amplitude modulation used for telephony, double sideband
a frequency modulation with on-off keying telegraphy of an audio frequency
dogru: an on-off keying of an amplitude modulated transmission used for aural telegraphy

Inside the ionosphere along the boundary layer
By line of sight between transmitter and receiver
dogru: Into space to be refracted in the ionosphere

soru: VHF propagates

secenekler: It depends on the altitude of the aircraft and the elevation of the ground antenna.
Obstacles like mountains between transmitter and aircraft do not affect the range.
It does not depend on the elevation of the antenna of the transmitter.
It does not depend on the altitude of the aircraft but only on the power of the transmitter.
dogru: It depends on the altitude of the aircraft and the elevation of the ground antenna.

the receiver moves away from the transmitter.
there is no relative movement between the transmitter and the receiver.
the transmitter moves away from the receiver.
dogru: the transmitter and receiver move towards each other.

soru: Soru metni bulunamadı:

there is no relative movement between the transmitter and the receiver.
the transmitter moves away from the receiver.
the transmitter moves toward the receiver.
dogru: the transmitter moves away from the receiver.

secenekler: the place where only the sky wave can be received.
the place where only the ground wave can be received.
the place where both ground and sky waves can be received.
the place where neither the ground waves nor the sky waves can be received.
dogru: the place where neither the ground waves nor the sky waves can be received.

soru: The skip distance is:
secenekler: the distance from transmitter to the maximum range of the ground wave
the distance from transmitter to earth surface up to the point where the first sky wave can be received
the distance from transmitter to an obstacle which limits the extension of the ground wave
the distance from transmitter to the maximum range of the sky wave
dogru: the distance from transmitter to earth surface up to the point where the first sky wave can be received

secenekler: from the beginning of evening twilight until the end of morning twilight.
dogru: from the beginning of evening twilight until the end of morning twilight.

soru: What is meant by the term 'Fading'?
secenekler: The interference of the ground and sky wave.
The disappearance of the D-layer with beginning of the evening twilight.
An effect which can be observed only in the F-layer of the ionosphere.
An effect at RADAR which leads to ‘blind speed’.
dogru: The interference of the ground and sky wave.

soru: Under what conditions can a VHF transmission often be received at a range considerably greater than line-of-sight?
secenekler: The signal may be reflected from mountains between the transmitter and receiver.
A temperature inversion in the lower atmosphere can cause ‘super-refraction’.
The signal may be refracted from the ionosphere at night.
The signal may be refracted as a surface wave over a large expanse of water.
dogru: A temperature inversion in the lower atmosphere can cause ‘super-refraction’.

dogru: The information is added onto the carrier wave by altering the frequency of the carrier.

dogru: A wave transmitted into space will be reflected back to the earth’s surface by the ionosphere.

to allow the signal to be amplified to the required power level.

secenekler: The wavelength of a Non-Directional Beacon (NDB) with a frequency of 300 kHz is...

soru: 1 metre
secenekler: orientation of the plane of oscillation of the magnetic component of the electromagnetic wave.
